LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO & MICHAEL HAASE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, TO STATE THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE CITY'S LIABILITY. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO BASE THE CITY'S LIABILITY OF VICARIOUS LIABILITY, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO IDENTIFY ACTS OR OMISSIONS OF EMPLOYEES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL HAASE, and DENNIS HERRERA'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend because plaintiffs failed to comply with the California Government Claims Act. The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. The DMV documents show (and Plaintiff concedes) that Entertainment Promotion, Inc. is the legal owner of the vehicle that was damaged. Entertainment Promotion, Inc. did not file a government claim and it cannot rely on Mr. Hunte's claim to support a cause of action. See Nguyen v. Los Angeles Cnty. Harbor/UCLA Med. Ctr. (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 729, 733-734. Mr. Hunte cannot recover for personal injury because his government claim did not cite any facts regarding personal injury and the government claim was limited to property damage. See Fall River Joint Unified School Dist. v. Sup. Crt. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 431, 434. The Court signed the order submitted by email. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To Apr-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL HAASE AND DENNIS HERRERA'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is continued to April 28, 2014 to give Plaintiff Marc Hunte the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and to permit CCSF the opportunity to file a reply to the opposition that was late-filed on March 24, 2014. Mr. Hunte must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 4, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any reply shall be filed and served in accordance with CCP 1005(b) based on the new hearing date. The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976

Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Continued To Aug-28-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Continued To Jul-18-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL HAASE and DENNIS HERRERA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued on the court's own motion to July 18, 2013 to permit CCSF the opportunity to file a reply to Mr. Hunte's opposition and separate request for a continuance late-filed on June 14, 2013. Mr. Hunte has not filed a proof of service of these filings, and Mr. Hunte is instructed to serve by mail the opposition and request for continuance filed on June 14, 2013 on CCSF no later than June 21, 2013. RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO & MICHAEL HAASE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. No opposition filed. To the extent plaintiff seeks to base the City's liability of vicarious liability, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to identify acts or omissions of employees. Order signed in open court.
